---
title: "Semigroup"
generated: true
description: "A binary operation supporting NumPy-style broadcasting."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.semiring`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.semiring._types`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Semigroup -->
```python
class Semigroup(Protocol)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Semigroup -->
A binary operation supporting NumPy-style broadcasting.

Used as the semiring's `(*)`: the per-$(i, k, j)$ combine
inside the contraction. Need not be commutative. Need not be
associative (it is the *outer* $+$ reduction that imposes the
algebraic structure; see [`Monoid`](../nitrix-semiring/Monoid.qmd)).

The kernel only relies on the broadcast call
`combine(a_col, b_row) -> value`, so `combine` should be a
pure JAX function with no captured state.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Semigroup.combine -->
```python
combine(a: Num[Array, '*shape'], b: Num[Array, '*shape']) -> Num[Array, '*shape']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Combine two operands elementwise with broadcasting.


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:Semigroup -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*shape` | a variadic group of axes (`shape`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
